text: Does your organisation maintain a complete, up-to-date inventory of all data sources, applications, and services (including cloud and third-party systems) that are treated as protected resources?
Are AI/ML models, model APIs, and training datasets explicitly classified and inventoried as protected resources, in the same way as databases or file servers?
When a new application, API, or third-party integration is added, is it formally registered as a resource before it is granted any access to your network or data?
Is all communication between users, services, and applications encrypted in transit, regardless of whether the connection originates inside or outside your network perimeter?
Do you enforce mutual authentication (not just one-directional trust) between internal services, including between an AI application and the databases or APIs it calls?
Are communications with third-party AI model providers or inference APIs secured and monitored to the same standard as internal traffic?
Is access to sensitive resources granted only for the duration of a single session, with re-verification required for each new session rather than a persistent standing access grant?
If a user or service account is compromised mid-session, can that specific session be revoked immediately without waiting for a scheduled credential expiry?

For AI-integrated workflows (e.g. an LLM calling internal tools or APIs on a user's behalf), is each individual tool call treated as its own access decision rather than inheriting blanket session trust?
Are access decisions made dynamically based on real-time context (user role, device health, location, behaviour), rather than static, pre-assigned permissions?
Does your access policy engine account for anomalous request patterns (e.g. unusually high query volume against a model API) and adjust access in response?
Are third-party and open-source AI components (pre-trained models, libraries, datasets) subject to a defined approval policy before they are integrated into production systems?
Do you continuously monitor the integrity and security posture of devices, servers, and applications, rather than relying on periodic manual audits?
note: T5 rated Partial or None against most AI-specific tactics in the matrix (e.g. Prompt Injection, Impact) - questions here should distinguish general asset monitoring from AI-behaviour monitoring.

text: Is the output behaviour of AI/LLM systems monitored for anomalies (e.g. unexpected instructions being followed, unusual response patterns) as part of your integrity monitoring programme?
Do you verify the cryptographic integrity (hashes, signatures, provenance) of third-party models, datasets, and dependencies before they are deployed?
Is multi-factor authentication enforced for all users and service accounts accessing sensitive systems, including AI/ML infrastructure and model APIs?
Are authentication and authorisation decisions re-evaluated continuously during a session (risk-based/adaptive authentication), rather than only at initial login?
Do non-human identities (service accounts, AI agents, automated pipelines) go through the same strict authentication requirements as human users?
Does your organisation collect and centralise telemetry on network state, asset health, and user/service behaviour to inform security decisions?
Are inputs and outputs of AI systems (prompts, responses, tool calls) logged in sufficient detail to support incident investigation after the fact?
Is posture and telemetry data from AI/ML pipelines fed back into your broader security monitoring and policy systems, rather than kept siloed within the AI/data science team?
